vocabulary.usage_note: Fixed expression indicating being at home (static location). Contrast: nach Hause = going home (movement toward).
Used to express surprise, admiration, or mild reaction. Often combined: Oh ja! (Oh yes!) / Oh nein! (Oh no!).
Modal particle that softens commands or suggestions: Schau mal! (Just look!). Very common in spoken German — has no direct Spanish/English translation.
Indicates location (there) or can mean 'here' in context: Schau mal, da! (Just look, over there!). Can also mean 'since/because' in subordinate clauses (later A2 topic).
Used in price questions: Wie viel kostet das? (How much does that cost?). Two-word form. Sometimes written 'wieviel' in older texts.
Used as a modal particle in questions to add curiosity or softness: Wie viel kostet er denn? (How much does it cost, then?) — more engaging than without 'denn'.
Limits or restricts: Das kostet nur 10 Euro. (That costs only 10 euros.) Spanish equivalent: 'solo/solamente'.
Intensifier meaning 'really/truly': Das ist wirklich schön! (That is really beautiful!). Spanish: 'verdaderamente/de verdad'.
Used before adjectives to mean 'too' (excessively): zu teuer (too expensive), zu klein (too small). Different from the preposition 'zu' (to) — context determines meaning.
Intensifier: Das Bild ist so schön! (The picture is so beautiful!). Also means 'so' in 'so … wie' comparisons (A2). Spanish: 'tan'.

examples.german: Ich bin zu Hause und schaue die neuen Möbel an.
examples.english: I am at home and looking at the new furniture.
examples.note: Uses: zu Hause (Lektion 4 phrase); schauen (Lektion 4 verb); die Möbel (Lektion 4 noun)

examples.german: Das Sofa ist so schön – ich möchte es sofort kaufen!
examples.english: The sofa is so beautiful – I want to buy it right away!
examples.note: Uses: so (Lektion 4 adverb); schön (Lektion 4 adjective); das Sofa (Lektion 4 noun)

examples.german: Das Bett kostet nur 99 Euro – das ist wirklich günstig!
examples.english: The bed costs only 99 euros – that is really cheap!
examples.note: Uses: nur, wirklich (Lektion 4 adverbs); günstig (Lektion 4 adjective); das Bett, der Euro (Lektion 4 nouns)

examples.german: Schau mal, da! Der Tisch kostet nur 50 Euro.
examples.english: Just look, over there! The table costs only 50 euros.
examples.note: Uses: mal, da, nur (Lektion 4 adverbs/particles); schauen (Lektion 4 verb); der Tisch, der Euro (Lektion 4 nouns)

examples.german: Wie viel kostet das Sofa denn? Es sieht so teuer aus!
examples.english: How much does the sofa cost, then? It looks so expensive!
examples.note: Uses: wie viel, denn, so (Lektion 4 others); das Sofa (Lektion 4 noun); teuer (Lektion 4 adjective)

examples.german: Ich bin zu Hause und finde das Zimmer zu klein für die neuen Möbel.
examples.english: I am at home and find the room too small for the new furniture.
examples.note: Uses: zu Hause (phrase), zu (adverb before adjective) — both Lektion 4 others; klein (adjective); die Möbel (noun)

examples.german: Oh, das ist wirklich ein Sonderangebot! Das Sofa kostet nur 200 Euro.
examples.english: Oh, that is really a special offer! The sofa costs only 200 euros.
examples.note: Uses: oh (interjection), wirklich, nur (adverbs) — Lektion 4 others; das Sonderangebot, das Sofa, der Euro (Lektion 4 nouns)

examples.german: Das Hotel ist so modern und das Zimmer ist wirklich groß – aber es ist zu teuer!
examples.english: The hotel is so modern and the room is really big – but it is too expensive!
examples.note: Uses: so, wirklich, zu (Lektion 4 adverbs); modern, groß, teuer (Lektion 4 adjectives); das Hotel, das Zimmer (Lektion 4 nouns)

examples.german: Schau mal da! Das Regal kostet nur 15 Euro – das ist wirklich günstig.
examples.english: Just look there! The shelf costs only 15 euros – that is really affordable.
examples.note: Uses: mal, da, nur, wirklich (four Lektion 4 adverbs); günstig (adjective); das Regal, der Euro (nouns)

examples.german: Ich bin zu Hause, und ich finde das Sofa so schön – aber es ist wirklich zu teuer.
examples.english: I am at home, and I find the sofa so beautiful – but it is really too expensive.
examples.note: Uses: zu Hause, so, wirklich, zu — multiple Lektion 4 others in one sentence

exercises.prompt: Ich bin ___ ___. (at home — two words)
exercises.explanation: zu Hause = at home (fixed two-word phrase). Contrast: nach Hause = going home (direction).

exercises.prompt: Das Sofa ist ___ schön! (so/such — intensifier)
exercises.explanation: so = so (intensifier before adjectives). Das Sofa ist so schön! = The sofa is so beautiful!

exercises.prompt: Das Bett kostet ___ 80 Euro. (only)
exercises.explanation: nur = only/just (limiting adverb). Spanish: solo/solamente.

exercises.prompt: Das ist ___ günstig! Nur 10 Euro! (really)
exercises.explanation: wirklich = really/truly. Intensifier: 'That is really cheap!'

exercises.prompt: Das Zimmer ist ___ klein für drei Personen. (too — excessively)
exercises.explanation: zu before an adjective = too (excessively): zu klein = too small.

exercises.prompt: Schau ___, ___! Der Schrank ist so schön. (just; over there)
exercises.explanation: Schau mal, da! = Just look, over there! 'mal' softens the imperative; 'da' indicates location.

exercises.prompt: ___ ___ kostet das Sofa? (how much — two words)
exercises.explanation: Wie viel = how much (price question). Wie viel kostet das Sofa? = How much does the sofa cost?

exercises.prompt: Wie viel kostet er ___? (then — modal particle in question)
exercises.explanation: 'denn' as a modal particle in questions adds curiosity/engagement. No direct translation — makes the question softer and more conversational.

exercises.prompt: ___, das ist sehr teuer! (Oh — exclamation)
exercises.explanation: 'Oh' is an interjection expressing surprise. Very common in German reactions.

exercises.prompt: Das Sofa ist ___ teuer. Ich kaufe es nicht. (too — excessively)
exercises.explanation: zu before adjective = too: zu teuer = too expensive.

exercises.prompt: Ich bin ___ ___ und schaue die Möbel an. (at home)
exercises.explanation: zu Hause = at home. Fixed phrase — must memorize. Cannot be separated or reordered.

exercises.prompt: Das Hotel ist ___ modern und ___ günstig! Ich buche es! (so; really)
exercises.explanation: so = so (intensifier); wirklich = really. Both adverbs intensify the adjectives.

exercises.prompt: Ich bin ___ ___. (I am at home)
exercises.explanation: zu Hause = at home (static location). 'nach Hause' = going home (direction). 'im Hause' is grammatically valid but not the fixed expression.

exercises.prompt: Das Bett kostet ___ 50 Euro. (only)
exercises.explanation: nur = only (limits: just 50 euros). 'wirklich' = really; 'so' = so.

exercises.prompt: Schau ___, da! Das Sofa ist so schön!
exercises.explanation: 'Schau mal' = just look. 'mal' softens the imperative. 'denn' is for questions; 'nur' means only.

exercises.prompt: Wie viel kostet das Hotel ___?
exercises.explanation: 'denn' in a question adds curiosity/engagement. 'nur' would change meaning to 'only'; 'mal' goes with imperatives.

exercises.prompt: Das Sofa ist ___ teuer für mich. Ich habe nicht so viel Geld.
exercises.explanation: zu before adjective = too (excessively): zu teuer = too expensive. Context: not enough money confirms 'too expensive'.

exercises.prompt: Das Zimmer ist ___ schön! Ich finde es fantastisch.
exercises.explanation: 'so schön' = so beautiful (positive intensifier). 'zu schön' would mean 'too beautiful' (a negative excess). Context: finding it fantastic → positive → 'so'.

exercises.prompt: I am at home.
exercises.explanation: zu Hause = at home. Fixed phrase. 'zu' here is not the preposition 'to' — it is part of this set expression.

exercises.prompt: Just look! The sofa is only 100 euros.
exercises.solution: Schau mal! Das Sofa kostet nur 100 Euro.
exercises.explanation: Schau mal! = Just look! (imperative + mal). nur = only. das Sofa kostet.

exercises.prompt: How much does the wardrobe cost?
exercises.explanation: Wie viel kostet...? Standard price question. der Schrank (masc.).

exercises.prompt: That is really beautiful!
exercises.explanation: wirklich = really/truly (intensifier). schön = beautiful.

exercises.prompt: The room is really too small for the furniture.
exercises.solution: Das Zimmer ist wirklich zu klein für die Möbel.
exercises.explanation: wirklich (really) + zu (too) + klein (small). die Möbel (furniture, plural-only noun).

exercises.prompt: I am at home and I find the picture so beautiful.
exercises.solution: Ich bin zu Hause und ich finde das Bild so schön.
exercises.explanation: zu Hause (at home), so (so — intensifier), schön (beautiful). das Bild (neut., Akkusativ unchanged).

exercises.prompt: How much does the lamp cost, then? It is really only 20 euros!
exercises.solution: Wie viel kostet die Lampe denn? Sie kostet wirklich nur 20 Euro!
exercises.explanation: denn (modal particle in question), wirklich (really), nur (only). die Lampe → sie (3rd person fem. pronoun).

exercises.prompt: Ich bin nach Hause. (context: I am sitting at home right now)
exercises.explanation: 'nach Hause' means going home (direction/movement). For static location at home: 'zu Hause'.

exercises.prompt: Das Bett kostet nur 50 Euro. Das ist so günstig! → is 'so' correctly used here?
exercises.solution: Correct — no error. 'So günstig' = so affordable (positive intensifier).
exercises.explanation: 'so' before an adjective as a positive intensifier is correct: 'so günstig' = so cheap.

exercises.prompt: Das Zimmer ist wirklich zu groß. (context: the room is so small you can barely fit a bed)
exercises.solution: Das Zimmer ist wirklich zu klein. (groß → klein)
exercises.explanation: Context says barely fits a bed → the room is too small → zu klein, not zu groß.

exercises.prompt: Wie wieviel kostet das Sofa?
exercises.explanation: 'wie viel' is two words (or written 'wieviel'). 'Wie wieviel' repeats the interrogative incorrectly.

exercises.prompt: Schau da mal! Der Schrank ist wirklich günstig.
exercises.solution: Schau mal, da! Der Schrank ist wirklich günstig.
exercises.explanation: The standard word order is 'Schau mal, da!' (Look, just over there!) — 'mal' directly follows the verb. However, 'Schau da mal' is acceptable colloquially. The conventional order is 'mal' before 'da'.

exercises.prompt: Das Hotel kostet zu 200 Euro. (context: 200 euros is too expensive)
exercises.solution: Das Hotel kostet 200 Euro. Das ist zu teuer.
exercises.explanation: 'zu' modifies adjectives, not prices directly. Correct: Das kostet 200 Euro — das ist zu teuer.

exercises.prompt: ___ ja! Das Sofa ist wirklich schön! (Oh — exclamation of agreement)
exercises.explanation: 'Oh ja!' = 'Oh yes!' — common exclamation expressing enthusiastic agreement or delight.

exercises.prompt: Schau mal ___! Der Teppich ist so günstig. (over there)
exercises.explanation: 'da' = there/over there. 'Schau mal da!' = Just look over there!

exercises.prompt: The sofa is really only 150 euros — how much is the armchair then?
exercises.solution: Das Sofa kostet wirklich nur 150 Euro — wie viel kostet der Sessel denn?
exercises.explanation: wirklich (really), nur (only), wie viel...denn? (how much...then? — question with modal particle).

exercises.prompt: ___ nein! Das Sofa ist hässlich! (Oh — exclamation of disappointment)
exercises.explanation: 'Oh nein!' = 'Oh no!' — interjection of disappointment or surprise.

exercises.prompt: Das Bild ist ___ schön! Ich kaufe es sofort.
exercises.explanation: Buying it immediately shows genuine admiration → wirklich (really). 'zu schön' (too beautiful) would be unexpected; 'denn' goes in questions.

exercises.prompt: I am at home and I only have 50 euros for the room.
exercises.solution: Ich bin zu Hause und ich habe nur 50 Euro für das Zimmer.
exercises.explanation: zu Hause (at home), nur (only), das Zimmer (room — neuter Akkusativ after für).

exercises.prompt: Das ist ___ günstig — ___ 5 Euro! (really; only)
exercises.explanation: wirklich = really (intensifier); nur = only (limiter). Both adverbs appear in natural pricing contexts.

exercises.prompt: Ich gehe zu Hause. (context: I am walking home right now)
exercises.explanation: 'nach Hause' = going home (direction with movement verb). 'zu Hause' = being at home (static).

exercises.prompt: Das Zimmer ist ___ groß. Es hat nur ein Bett. (too — excessively small? No: read carefully — only one bed = it's too small)
exercises.explanation: Only one bed in the room implies it is too small → zu klein. 'zu' before adjective = too.

exercises.prompt: Look there! That is really a special offer.
exercises.solution: Schau mal da! Das ist wirklich ein Sonderangebot.
exercises.explanation: Schau mal da! = Look (just) there! wirklich = really. das Sonderangebot (neut.).

exercises.prompt: Wie viel kostet das Sofa ___? Ich möchte es kaufen.
exercises.explanation: 'denn' in a question signals genuine curiosity/interest. 'mal' is for imperatives; 'nur' would change the meaning to 'only'.

exercises.prompt: Das Hotel ist ___ teuer und ___ klein. Ich buche ein anderes. (too; too — use same word twice)
exercises.explanation: 'zu' before each adjective = too expensive AND too small. The same particle is used twice.
